title: The 2015 Movie Draft. Plus: Amanda Seyfried!
format: Sean, Amanda, and Chris Ryan snake-draft the movies of 2015 across six categories, then Sean talks to Amanda Seyfried about playing Marion Davies in Mank.

segments.heading: Cold open: Godzilla vs. Kong and a Snyder Cut watch-along tease

films.title: Godzilla vs. Kong
films.note: The trailer dialogue gets read as some of the best writing Sean's heard in years; Chris compares it to shit-town Kyle Chandler yelling that Godzilla is angry and nobody knows why.

films.title: Godzilla
films.note: The Gareth Edwards/Bryan Cranston one. Amanda's contemporaneous verdict, texted to Chris after seeing it: 'don't see it. We will not be discussing it on the podcast.'

films.title: Godzilla: King of the Monsters
films.note: Michael Dougherty's sequel, with Vera Farmiga.

films.title: Kong: Skull Island
films.note: An important one for Sean and Chris, who read it as a Vietnam movie minus the actual Vietnam-movie part. John C. Reilly gets singled out.

films.title: Zack Snyder's Justice League
films.note: Chris pitches recording a live four-hour commentary track for the eventual HBO Max cut; Sean is not on board with prepping, just drinking milk.

picks.title: The Hateful Eight
picks.note: Not his favorite Tarantino, but far better than almost anything else in theaters that year. Prefers the extended, chapterized Netflix roadshow cut.

picks.title: It Follows
picks.note: One of the best horror movies of the last decade for him: David Robert Mitchell's dread about STDs and a hollowed-out urban landscape. Says the pandemic has given it new resonance, and calls Maika Monroe the new Jamie Lee Curtis.

picks.title: Mission: Impossible - Rogue Nation
picks.note: Christopher McQuarrie's first in the series, before he'd go on to direct Fallout. Credits it with reviving the franchise and introducing Rebecca Ferguson.

picks.title: Inside Out
picks.note: One of his favorite movies, period. Says he had a full evangelizing reaction in the theater. Pete Docter also just co-directed Soul.

picks.title: Creed
picks.note: Ryan Coogler's Rocky follow-up. Amanda's husband apparently has a rare bad take that the movie isn't good; Sean's rewatched it and still loves it.

picks.title: Mississippi Grind
picks.note: Amanda and Chris talk him into it. He calls it basically a remake of California Split, a top-ten movie of all time for him.

picks.title: Sicario
picks.note: His pick for best movie of the year, punctuated by the recurring bit about a stranger yelling 'Sicario!' out a car window at him in 2015.

picks.title: The Big Short
picks.note: One of his most-rewatched movies of the last five years thanks to its Netflix run. Loves the vignette structure and Ryan Gosling's Jared Vennett; Steve Carell's central performance is the one part that doesn't land for him.

picks.title: Ant-Man
picks.note: Breaks his stated plan to avoid drafting any MCU movies. Calls it a disposable but fun Paul Rudd/Michael Douglas heist comedy that made $190 million anyway.

picks.title: Güeros
picks.note: A Mexican road movie directed by Alonso Ruizpalacios, described as a French New Wave-inflected portrait of 1999 Mexico City. Says it's grown in his estimation on a recent rewatch.

picks.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens
picks.note: Called out by the others as the draft's first bad pick. He defends it as a fun romp built on the nostalgia of finally seeing Harrison Ford back as Han Solo.

picks.title: Steve Jobs
picks.note: Aaron Sorkin's script; a movie he says was overlooked on release but has built a growing cult following since.

picks.title: Spotlight
picks.note: Saw it alone at BAM; singles out the closing title card tallying the places the investigation spread to as a gut punch. Notes it also just won Best Picture.

picks.title: The Intern
picks.note: Nancy Meyers; likes the Robert De Niro/Anne Hathaway chemistry and reads it as a kind of response to Meyers's own Baby Boom.

picks.title: The Martian
picks.note: A one-man-show Matt Damon performance she loves without reservation: 'you're just like, sure, I'll watch you grow potatoes for a while.'

picks.title: Clouds of Sils Maria
picks.note: Triple-checked the US release date against a New York Times review before taking it, given how messy foreign-film release dates get. Praises Kristen Stewart's performance opposite Juliette Binoche.

picks.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
picks.note: Takes it with the very first pick of the draft. All three call it one of the best movies of the decade; ends up crowned the draft's overall winner.

picks.title: Focus
picks.note: A Will Smith/Margot Robbie con-artist movie she says is underrated: charming leads, plausible enough heist mechanics.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Plays Marion Davies opposite Gary Oldman's Herman Mankiewicz. Talks through getting the script in pieces on another set, David Fincher's rehearsal process, and shooting the long Sans Simeon walk-and-talk.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: The film Mank is about the writing of; Seyfried talks about how Davies's real legacy got flattened by Kane's version of her.

films.title: Ever Since Eve
films.note: One of the real Marion Davies pictures Seyfried rewatched to prep, singling out an elevator-hallway scene opposite Robert Montgomery.

films.title: Lovelace
films.note: Where she played another real person, Linda Lovelace; comes up when discussing the line between impersonation and embodiment.

films.title: Chloe
films.note: Cites it as a deliberate swing against typecasting, taken specifically because Atom Egoyan was directing.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: Calls it one of her absolute favorite films; part of the case Sean makes that she could've coasted as a rom-com lead but chased directors like Paul Schrader instead.

films.title: Pieces of a Woman
films.note: Her answer to the last-great-thing-she's-seen question. Says the birth scene is as real as anything she's seen that isn't real, and singles out Shia LaBeouf's performance.
